name: Cod with Garlic
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the cod fillets and pat dry.
2. Place the fillets in a glass or enamel coated dish with 2-inch sides, and sprinkle with lime juice. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 1 hour.
3. In a large heavy skillet, combine olive oil and butter and heat over medium heat. Add garlic and sauté only until light brown; be careful not to burn the dish will be bitter. Using a slotted spoon, remove the garlic and set aside; remove pan from the heat.
4. Remove fish from the dish, reserving lime juice, and pat dry with a paper towel. Dust lightly with flour and shake off excess. Warm skillet over medium-high heat and quickly fry fish 2-3 minutes per side, or until brown and crisp outside and just done inside. Remove fish to a heated platter and keep warm while finishing the dish.
5. Return garlic to skillet and reheat. Stir in reserved lime juice, cilantro, salt, and pepper to taste. Heat just until cilantro begins to wilt. Pour sauce over fish and serve immediately.
